final_text:
I have a clear answer. Let me verify the field names match exactly.

The sample has: `individualID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, `bodySite`

- The HTAN `Biospecimen` schema uses ALL_CAPS field names like `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `SITE_OF_RESECTION_OR_BIOPSY`, etc. — **none** of the sample's field names match.
- The NF-OSI `BiospecimenTemplate` schema includes `individualID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, and `bodySite` — **all four** sample fields match exactly.

## Schema identification

**Match: `BiospecimenTemplate`** (from the nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary repository)

- **Identifier (`$id`):** `https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate`
- **Title:** `BiospecimenTemplate`
- **Source path:** `nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary/registered-json-schemas/BiospecimenTemplate.json`

### Why this is the match
All four fields in the sample (`individualID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, `bodySite`) appear as properties of the NF-OSI `BiospecimenTemplate` schema, alongside related fields such as `parentSpecimenID`, `tumorType`, `experimentalCondition`, and several model-system fields. The sample appears to be a partial/subset instance — the schema defines additional properties not present in the sample, but no field in the sample is foreign to the schema.

### Why the HTAN candidate was ruled out
The HTAN v1.1.0 `Biospecimen` schema (`https://w3id.org/htan/biospecimen`) uses an entirely different field-naming convention (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, e.g., `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `SITE_OF_RESECTION_OR_BIOPSY`, `BIOSPECIMEN_TYPE`). None of the four sample field names appear in that schema, so it does not match.

### Caveat
I only inspected the obvious biospecimen-named schemas in each repo. I did not exhaustively check every one of the ~23 HTAN schemas or ~60 NF-OSI templates for these four common field names. However, given that `individualID` / `specimenID` / `aliquotID` / `bodySite` are the canonical NF-OSI biospecimen identifiers and the field naming convention rules out all HTAN v1.1.0 schemas (which uniformly use ALL_CAPS), I am confident in this identification.
grader_notes: Committed to BiospecimenTemplate citing all four fields including bodySite matching NF-OSI schema.
